REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MYND MANAGEMENT, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 8, 2024 ON COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MYND MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL EVICTION AND ELDER ABUSE IS OVERRULED AS THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CARRY THEIR BURDEN AND SHOW THAT COMPLAINT FAILS TO STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. LEGAL AUTHORITIES CITED IN THE RELEVANT SECTIONS OF THE DEMURRER ARE NOT ANALYZED, THE MISSING ELEMENTS ARE NOT DISCUSSED, ETC. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING ALL ELEMENTS OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE DETAILS OF THE FALSE PROMISE, INTENT TO INDUCE RELIANCE, ACTUAL RELIANCE; OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT; DUTY AND BREACH OF DUTY. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt, Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-21-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Myrna Van; 949-336-1200; Tressler LLP; counsel for defendant Mynd Management Inc.
James Chaffee; 415-656-9946; plaintiff in pro per.
Reporter: Tamra Elaine Keen; CSR# 5404; 619-546-9151; tkeencsf@gmail.com

Hearing held (See reporter's transcript for full details). Ms. Van contests the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MYND MANAGEMENT, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alterantive Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is denied as moving party failed to shift their burden. No competent evidence submitted in support of the motion. See Declaration of Giles Imrie.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Reported: Tamra Elaine Keen; CSR# 5404; 619-546-9151; tkeencsf@gmail.com; FTR (D501)

I, Lorina Young was designated by the San Francisco Superior Court as monitor for the electronic recording on 11/21/24 in Department 501. The recording equipment was functioning normally, and all proceedings in open court between designated times of the day were recorded, except for such matters as were expressly directed to be 'off the record' or as otherwise specified."

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Annie Barberian; Tressler LLP; 949-336-1200; appearing as counsel for defendant Mynd Management, Inc.

Defendant contests the overruled portions of the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MYND MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the causes of action for wrongful eviction and elder abuse is overruled as the moving party failed to carry their burden and show that complaint fails to state facts to support these causes of action. Legal authorities cited in the relevant sections of the demurrer are not analyzed, the missing elements are not discussed, etc. Demurrer to the causes of action for fraud, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege facts supporting all elements of these causes of action, including but not limited to the details of the false promise, intent to induce reliance, actual reliance; outrageous conduct; duty and breach of duty. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Annie Barberian; Tressler LLP; 949-336-1200; appearing as counsel for defendant Mynd Management, Inc.

Not argued. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MYND MANAGEMENT, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts in support of malice, oppression or fraud. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
